hitchens_question: When did we decide that the difference between "precision warfare" and "war crimes" was simply a matter of which satellites are doing the watching, and why do we pretend that our moral calculus changes based on whether the rubble in Rakhine, Lebanon, or Ukraine appears in our feeds with a time stamp that makes us feel appropriately concerned? The same technology that promises to liberate our wounded soldiers from their biological limitations is simultaneously perfecting the art of killing at distances that spare us any troubling intimacy with the consequences—so tell me, exactly when did we become a civilization that innovates most rapidly in the service of avoiding uncomfortable truths?
